taxonomy: Pseudoliparis undulata is a species of fish, and more specifically, it belongs to the family Liparidae, commonly known as snailfish. This classification places it within the larger group of perciform fishes. As a member of the Liparidae family, Pseudoliparis undulata shares characteristics with other snailfish species. The family Liparidae is further classified under the order Perciformes and the class Actinopterygii. Within the broader taxonomic hierarchy, Pseudoliparis undulata is also classified as a vertebrate.

intro: Pseudoliparis undulata, also known as Chinese club moss, is a type of lycopod plant that belongs to the family Lycopodiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, which can help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, wounds, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Pseudoliparis undulata include flavonoids, terpenoids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Pseudoliparis undulata is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, teas, and infusions, which are used to treat a variety of health conditions, including bronchitis, cough, and skin diseases.
